2.rootCause: The colored arc segments are not exposed as accessible interactive elements — the automated tester can only see the non-ring controls and cannot interact with the ring.
2.fixTarget: Expose the arc segments as real interactive targets: role='button', tabindex='0', key handlers; remove the a11y-flattening role='img' from the svg.

3.rootCause: The orbiting dot did not demonstrate observable movement across screenshots — cloud browsers may run with prefers-reduced-motion, where a static dot is the correct accessible behavior.
3.fixTarget: Refine the test plan to assert settled end-states, never continuous ambient motion (lesson L2). App unchanged.

5.rootCause: Checker returned status 'blocked' while its own conclusion read 'PASS — all checks succeeded' with zero failed steps.
5.fixTarget: Verdict recorded from the evidence (lesson L3); discrepancy reported upstream.

6.rootCause: The harness diagram's 11px 'pass' connector label was unreadable in the tester's screenshots; boxes and connectors verified, label check failed.
6.fixTarget: Meaning-carrying labels at ≥13px SVG (lesson L4); raised to 13.5px weight-500, rerun verified the label.

7.rootCause: The tester clicked the replay rail's diamond ('Seek to iteration 2') instead of the ring arc — two controls shared the entity name.
7.fixTarget: Diverge accessible names and name the widget type in plans (lesson L5); rerun passed 17/17.

9.rootCause: At 8 iterations the per-segment intro left arcs invisible 3+ seconds; the tester indexed mid-intro and found no clickable ring, then missed SVG-child controls intermittently.
9.fixTarget: Intro capped at a 0.9s total budget (lesson L6) and controls hardened into real HTML button hit-targets; rerun verified 4/4.

13.rootCause: The 'press ? for shortcuts' hint carried aria-label='Open keyboard shortcuts', which overrides the visible text in the accessibility tree — so the a11y-tree-reading cloud tester never saw the '?' the test needed to confirm.
13.fixTarget: Remove the masking aria-label so the visible text is the accessible name (lesson L7 — third of the a11y trio after L1 and L5).
